content.full_text:
July 26, 2002

Dana Graves Ralls
245 West K Street
Benicia, CA 94510

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-188

Dear Ms. Ralls:

Pursuant to your telephone conversation with our office on July 24, 2002, this letter confirms that you are withdrawing your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Your request pertained to our file No. 01-095, wherein Ms. McLaughlin, City Attorney for the City of Benicia, sought our advice on your behalf while you were under consideration for appointment to the city’s Historic Preservation Commission. On September 18, 2001, Ms. McLaughlin’s request for this advice was deemed withdrawn.1 In a telephone conversation occurring on July 25, 2002, you informed the Commission’s staff that the seat on the Historic Preservation Commission was filled with a different appointment.

Please note that the Commission does not offer advice or assistance addressing purely hypothetical questions. (Regulations 18329(b)(8)(D) and 18329(c)(4)(D), copy enclosed.) However, should you again be under consideration for a future opening on the city’s Historic Preservation Commission (as you indicate might occur), you may write to us directly, or authorize the city’s attorney to write to us on your behalf, seeking our advice or assistance.

1 A copy of our letter No. 01-095 is enclosed for your information.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kenneth L. Gluck
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

cc: Ms. Heather C. McLaughlin, City Attorney
    City of Benicia
    City Hall
    251 East L Street
    Benicia, CA 94510

KLG:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-188
